benefits.disable: You can disable anytime in Settings

chainGuardian.backgroundHint: Works even when app is closed

transfer.title: Device Data Transfer
transfer.description: Move app settings and contacts to your new phone by scanning a transfer QR code.
transfer.warning: This transfers app data only. Wallet recovery phrase/private key are NOT included. Import wallet separately on the new phone.
transfer.exportHint: On your new phone, open Settings -> Device Data Transfer -> Scan Transfer QR, then scan this code.
transfer.exportHintParts: On your new phone, scan every page in order (1 to {{total}}), then enter the 6-digit code.
transfer.exportHintRelay: On your new phone, scan this one code to download the transfer package, then enter the 6-digit confirmation code.
transfer.exportReadyParts: Transfer QR ready ({{count}} items, {{parts}} QR pages). Scan pages in order.
transfer.relayReady: Large transfer optimized: scan one QR to download {{count}} items, then enter the 6-digit code.
transfer.relayFallback: Cloud transfer unavailable ({{message}}). Falling back to multi-page QR.
transfer.importHint: Point the camera at the transfer QR shown on your old phone.
transfer.imported: Imported {{count}} items. Wallet recovery phrase/private key are NOT transferred.
transfer.confirmPrompt: QR scanned. Enter the 6-digit confirmation code shown on your old phone.
transfer.confirmCodeRequired: Enter the 6-digit confirmation code from your old phone.
transfer.confirmCodeMismatch: Confirmation code does not match. Please try again.
transfer.scanContinue: Keep scanning remaining pages in order until complete.
transfer.chunkMismatch: Transfer QR pages do not match. Please restart scan from page 1.

verify.title: Verify You're Human
verify.description: To receive daily rewards, verify that you're a unique human.

poh.subtitle: Unlock daily rewards
poh.description: Verify liveness with inLyf to unlock rewards. No biometrics are stored on-chain; only a short-lived credential is issued.
poh.howItWorksDesc: A short liveness check (face scan) verifies you are human. Your credential lasts 90 days and can be refreshed anytime.

status.expiredDetail: Re-run liveness to refresh your credential.

capture.tipFace: Keep your face centered and fill the frame (≥ {{pct}}%).
capture.tipLight: Use even lighting and avoid strong backlight.

capture.tipRemove: Remove hats, masks, or heavy shadows.

capture.errorFileTooSmall: Video file is too small. Hold still for the full capture and improve lighting.
capture.errorBitrate: Video quality is too low. Improve lighting and keep the phone steady.
capture.errorLowQuality: Capture quality too low. Please use better lighting or a higher quality camera.
capture.errorCameraPermission: Camera permission is required. Enable it in Settings and try again.
capture.errorCameraUnavailable: Camera unavailable on this device. Close other camera apps and try again.
capture.errorRecordingFailed: Video recording failed. Please try again with better lighting.
capture.errorStorage: Not enough free storage to record video. Free up space and retry.
capture.errorFileAccess: Could not read the recorded video. Please retake.
capture.errorDeviceAttest: Device security check failed. Update Google Play services and retry.
capture.errorCooldown: Too many attempts. Please wait before trying again.
capture.errorChallenge: Active challenge not completed. Please retake and follow instructions.
capture.errorUpload: Upload failed. Check your connection and retry.
capture.errorUploadForbidden: Upload URL was rejected (expired or signature mismatch). Please retake and retry.
capture.errorUploadTimeout: Upload timed out. Check connection and retry.
capture.errorUploadServer: Upload service is temporarily unavailable. Please retry shortly.

sentinelSettings.header: Join the Sentinel pool to help finalize blocks. Bond at least the minimum amount, keep your device online, and earn rewards.

bond.createDescription: Bond UMT to join the sentinel committee and start earning.
bond.topUpDescription: Increase your stake to earn a larger share of rewards.

autoClaim.title: Auto-Claim
autoClaim.description: Let the relayer automatically claim your sentinel rewards when they accrue.

activity.title: Recent Activity
activity.noClaims: No claims in the last {{blocks}}k blocks.
activity.noSlashes: No slash events in the recent window.

errors.configureEnv: Configure RPC + sentinel contract env vars.
errors.noWallet: Create or import a wallet to join the sentinel committee.

toast.minBondRequired: Initial bond must be at least {{amount}} UMT

miniapps.title: MiniApps
miniapps.subtitle: Explore apps built on Umotu
miniapps.pohDescription: Verify your humanity with inLyf to unlock daily rewards.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: Umotu is an open protocol designed to make participation in a global digital economy simpler: open access, fast settlement, and optional stablecoins for everyday transactions.

ubi.title: Open participation, daily rewards
ubi.description: Daily rewards are earned through participation in network security. Rewards are variable and not guaranteed.

capital.title: Low-friction transfers
capital.description: Payments and transfers are designed to be fast and low-friction. Fees and performance vary with network conditions and the apps you use.

sovereignty.title: Compatible with local money
sovereignty.description: Umotu does not control monetary policy and does not replace fiat currencies. Stablecoins on Umotu are issued by third parties and have their own rules and risks.

vision.notReplacing: Umotu is infrastructure. It does not set monetary policy, does not guarantee stablecoin safety, and does not intervene to produce specific outcomes.

building.title: We're Building the Ecosystem
building.description: These apps showcase what's possible on Umotu. Some are ready now, others are coming soon. Developers worldwide are invited to build and launch on our platform.

sendMoney.name: Send Money
sendMoney.description: Send UMT or stablecoins instantly to anyone, anywhere in the world—no banks needed.
sendMoney.example: Send $20 to your family abroad in seconds, not days. Zero hidden fees.

billPay.name: Bill Pay
billPay.description: Pay utilities, phone, internet, and more directly from your wallet.

savings.name: Savings Circles
savings.description: Traditional rotating savings (tontine, chit fund) made trustless with smart contracts.

microLoans.name: Micro Loans
microLoans.description: Peer-to-peer lending backed by your verified humanity and reputation.

rideSharing.name: Ride Sharing
rideSharing.description: Book rides paid in crypto. Drivers keep more, riders pay less—no middleman taking 30%.
rideSharing.example: A taxi ride in Lagos costs ₦800. Driver gets ₦799.2 instead of ₦560.

delivery.name: Delivery
delivery.description: Order food, groceries, or packages. Couriers earn fair wages in stable tokens.
delivery.example: Order lunch, pay in your local stablecoin, courier gets paid instantly.

publicTransit.name: Public Transit
publicTransit.description: Pay for buses, metros, and trains with a tap. No need to buy fare cards.

hotels.name: Hotels & Stays
hotels.description: Book accommodations worldwide. Hosts accept stablecoins, guests save on fees.
hotels.example: Book a room in Bali for $40/night. Host receives $39.96.

flights.name: Flights
flights.description: Search and book flights. Pay in crypto, earn UMT rewards.

tours.name: Local Tours
tours.description: Book experiences with local guides. Payments go directly to them.

localMarket.name: Local Market
localMarket.description: Buy and sell locally. QR-based payments make street commerce seamless.
localMarket.example: A vendor in Jakarta accepts USDC. Customer pays, vendor sees funds instantly.

ecommerce.name: E-commerce
ecommerce.description: Shop online from merchants worldwide. No card fees, no chargebacks.
ecommerce.example: Buy electronics from Shenzhen. Pay in stablecoins, save 3% vs credit card.

marketplace.name: Peer Marketplace
marketplace.description: List items for sale, buy from others. Escrow protects both parties.

remittance.name: Remittance
remittance.description: Send money home. Recipients can cash out to local currency or keep it digital.
remittance.example: Send $200 from USA to Philippines. Recipient gets ₱11,000+ instead of ₱10,400.

freelance.name: Freelance Work
freelance.description: Get paid for gig work instantly. No waiting 30 days for bank transfers.

homeServices.name: Home Services
homeServices.description: Hire plumbers, electricians, cleaners. Pay only when the job is done.

business.title: Build Your Business on Umotu
business.subtitle: Launch apps with built-in payments
business.description: Build apps with on-chain payments and optional stablecoins. Participants may earn daily rewards (variable) and can transact using supported assets.

benefits.noFees: Low, transparent fees — exact costs vary with network conditions
benefits.verifiedUsers: Verified participants — reduced bot and Sybil activity via Proof of Human
benefits.instantPayments: Fast on-chain settlement — timing depends on network conditions

business.exampleText: A ride-sharing app can route payments directly between riders and drivers, with transparent fees and near-real-time settlement on-chain.

bank.title: Are You a Bank?
bank.subtitle: Issue stablecoins on Umotu
bank.description: Financial institutions and payment companies can issue stablecoins on Umotu to serve users who prefer stable unit-of-account assets for everyday transactions.

benefits.reserves: Reserve management is controlled by the issuer and subject to applicable rules
benefits.mintRedeem: Issuers may set mint/redeem fees at their discretion
benefits.users: Access a growing base of active participants
benefits.global: Reach users across borders (subject to compliance and distribution)

bank.revenueNote: Issuer economics vary. Some issuers generate revenue from reserve yield and/or mint/redeem fees, depending on their model and obligations.
bank.futureNote: Nothing in the protocol guarantees issuer revenue. Future changes, if any, must follow the protocol's governance process.

stablesDocs.title: Stablecoin Standards
stablesDocs.subtitle: Defaults for Umotu Foundation apps (not protocol permission)
stablesDocs.description: Umotu Foundation publishes standards used for default stablecoin support in Foundation-maintained apps and services. They do not restrict protocol-level transfers or third-party wallets. Standards do not eliminate stablecoin risk.

uss.title: Umotu Stablecoin Standard (USS)
uss.description: Minimum disclosure, reserve, and redemption criteria for stablecoins shown as default payout options and supported in stable-gas services (via paymasters) in Umotu Foundation apps.

rdp.title: Risk Disclosure Policy (RDP)
rdp.description: Rule-based risk indicators (e.g., FX volatility, peg deviations, redemption restrictions, and technical risk) shown in Umotu Foundation apps.

news.title: News & Updates
news.description: Stay updated with the latest announcements, tips, and security alerts from the Umotu team.

governance.title: Governance
governance.subtitle: Shape the future of Umotu

description.title: Your Voice Matters
description.body: Help shape the future of Umotu by voting on community proposals. The Umotu Foundation organizes these votes, but the community decides.

governance.emptyHint: Check back later for new community decisions to vote on.
governance.tapToVote: Tap an option above to cast your vote

alreadyVoted.title: Already Voted
alreadyVoted.message: You have already voted in this proposal.

success.title: Vote Submitted!
success.message: Thank you for participating in community governance.

error.title: Vote Failed
error.message: Failed to submit vote. Please try again.

whatIs.title: What is Umotu?
whatIs.subtitle: Open Participation, Daily Rewards
whatIs.intro: Umotu is an open protocol where anyone can participate using a smartphone and earn daily rewards for helping secure the network. Rewards are variable and not guaranteed. For orientation only, the protocol is designed around a reference target of about $10/day per active participant at launch, but actual rewards can be much lower (including pennies per day) or higher depending on participation and market conditions.
whatIs.noMiddleman: No middleman: you interact with the network directly—no account approval required
whatIs.globalAccess: Global access: works wherever you have internet
whatIs.youOwnIt: Self-custody: your wallet, your keys, your control
whatIs.visionText: Millions of phones can help keep a public ledger honest. Phones do lightweight checks (like data availability sampling) that make the network more trustworthy.
whatIs.nameOriginText: Umotu takes inspiration from Paʻumotu, a Polynesian language. We use the name respectfully as a reminder to build for everyday people.

earn.title: How You Earn
earn.subtitle: Your phone participates; you earn rewards
earn.intro: Your phone performs small security checks that help the network—then you earn rewards.
earn.step1: 1) Prove you're human: a quick liveness check (and occasional refresh) ensures each person has one account.
earn.step2: 2) Help the network: your phone runs Data Availability (DA) sampling and other lightweight checks in the background.
earn.step3: 3) Earn daily rewards: rewards are distributed automatically based on successful participation. Amounts vary.
earn.localCurrencyText: Choose a payout asset: UMT or stablecoins (where available). Stablecoins are issued by third parties and have their own risks.

whereMoney.title: Where Do Rewards Come From?
whereMoney.subtitle: You earn because you do work
whereMoney.intro: It’s a common question. The short answer: rewards exist because participants provide security, and the protocol issues tokens by rule.
whereMoney.realWork1: Data Availability Sampling: your phone checks that block data is available and correct
whereMoney.realWork2: Human Participation: you prove one-person-one-account to stop bots and spam
whereMoney.realWork3: These tasks protect everyone; without them the network weakens
whereMoney.utility: Utility: fast, low-fee payments people choose to use
whereMoney.consensus: Consensus: value that grows as more people can verify and agree on the rules
whereMoney.newTokens: New UMT tokens are created and shared with verified phone participants (not with validators)
whereMoney.fees: A portion of fees and builder bids is shared with phone sentinels for finalizing blocks
whereMoney.treasury: Some fees may support ecosystem maintenance (e.g., audits and reference software) through transparent governance, where enabled.
whereMoney.umotuSolution: Umotu rewards everyday phones (low practical cost to participate)
whereMoney.conclusion: It’s not a giveaway: you’re paid for work that keeps a shared financial infrastructure honest and useful.

security.title: Wallet Security
security.subtitle: Protect your money
security.intro: Your wallet is like a digital vault that only you control.
security.recoveryText: Your 12 secret words (recovery phrase) and your private key both unlock your wallet. Anyone who has either can move your funds.
security.rule1: NEVER share your 12 words or private key with anyone—not even Umotu contributors.
security.rule2: Write your 12 words on paper and store them offline. Do not store them in screenshots, photos, or cloud notes.
security.rule3: If you export your private key, keep it offline (paper, hardware, or an encrypted manager you fully trust).
security.rule4: If you lose your 12 words and private key, no one can recover your wallet.
security.warningText: Your private key grants full access. Revealing or copying it to other apps can expose your funds. Only export if you understand the risk.
security.revealText: You can reveal or export your private key in Wallet Settings. We require biometrics/PIN, show it only for a few seconds, and never upload it. Consider using a hardware wallet for large balances.
security.best1: Enable screen lock & biometrics on your phone.
security.best2: Keep the app updated to get the latest security fixes.
security.best3: Use a separate wallet for large savings; keep small amounts on your phone for daily use.
security.scamBannerText: If anyone asks for your 12 words or private key, it’s a scam.

stablecoins.title: Understanding Stablecoins
stablecoins.subtitle: Digital money that tracks local currency
stablecoins.intro: Stablecoins are digital tokens designed to track regular money (like USD, ZAR, NGN).
stablecoins.backedText: Stablecoins are issued by independent providers and are designed to track a currency (like USD or CAD). Each stablecoin has its own reserves, redemption terms, upgrade controls, and risk policies (including possible freezing). Read the issuer’s disclosures.
stablecoins.convertText: You can swap stablecoins on-chain, or convert through third-party services where available.
stablecoins.gasText: Some wallets can let you pay network fees using stablecoins through a service that swaps to the native token. Validators still receive the native token; stable-gas is a convenience layer.
stablecoins.preferredListText: The Umotu wallet may show a conservative default list of stablecoins for payouts and fee services based on the Umotu Stablecoin Standard (USS). Other wallets can support other stablecoins.

economics.title: UMT Economics
economics.subtitle: Who gets what and why
economics.intro: UMT has a fixed supply of 100 billion tokens. Most of it is reserved for phone participants over time, distributed by a rule-based emission controller.
supplyBullets: The vast majority of the supply is reserved for phone participants and released over time through an Emission Controller. The controller caps daily issuance at no more than 0.01% of the remaining supply to limit dilution.
At the maximum cap (0.01% of remaining supply per day), about 69% of the supply remains after 10 years and about 16% remains after 50 years. Actual emission can be lower depending on protocol rules.
economics.premine: Launch Allocation (tentative, subject to change at mainnet launch)
economics.emissionsText: Daily emissions are capped. Your rewards depend on participation, successful phone checks, and market conditions. The protocol does not promise a fixed amount.
economics.feesText: Sentinel helpers (optional, advanced) earn a share of transaction fees and builder bids. This is separate from daily emissions.
economics.transparencyText: Vesting schedules and wallets are public. Daily emission caps and realized payouts are posted in the app.
economics.disclaimerText: This is not financial advice. Rewards are variable and not guaranteed.

contribute.title: How to Contribute
contribute.subtitle: Help build Umotu
contribute.intro: Umotu is open source and community-driven. Everyone can help—whether you code, translate, or simply share feedback.

code.title: Improve the Code
code.text: Fix bugs, add features, write tests, or review pull requests. Check our GitHub repos for the app, smart contracts, and backend services.

translate.title: Help with Translations
translate.text: Make the app feel natural in your language. Edit the i18n locale files and test every screen. Your edits help millions.

wordlists.title: Expand BIP-39 Languages
wordlists.text: Propose new or improved wordlists so more people can see their 12-word recovery phrase in their native language.

governance.title: Join Governance
governance.text: Read proposals, discuss openly, and vote in the app. Votes can guide wallet defaults and ecosystem grants. Some protocol rules are fixed and not up for vote.

community.title: Spread the Word
community.text: Tell friends, write tutorials, publish shorts, answer questions. A stronger community means a stronger network for everyone.

contribute.note: All contributions are voluntary. There is no formal employment or compensation unless specified in a grant proposal.

decentralization.title: Why Decentralization
decentralization.subtitle: No single point of control
decentralization.intro: Decentralization means the network is run by many independent participants. Your wallet is self-custodial—only your keys can move your funds.
decentralization.benefit2: Often lower fees: small network fees instead of typical card fees
decentralization.benefit4: More censorship-resistant: no single company controls your wallet
decentralization.benefit5: Transparent: anyone can verify how the system works
decentralization.howWorksText: Validators propose blocks every ~2 seconds. Phones (like yours) verify data availability and help finalize blocks with lightweight checks. Many small contributions add up to strong security.

noCompany.title: Open Protocol, Self-Custody
noCompany.subtitle: Understand what Umotu is (and isn’t)
noCompany.intro: There is no single company controlling the protocol. Umotu is an open set of rules anyone can use, and many independent teams can build wallets and services.
noCompany.notCompany1: NOT a custodial bank holding customer funds
noCompany.notCompany2: NOT able to reset your wallet or reverse transactions
noCompany.notCompany3: NOT controlled by any single entity
noCompany.whatIs1: A protocol: open, interoperable rules (like email or the web)
noCompany.whatIs2: Phone-first participation: smartphones can help secure the network
noCompany.whatIs3: Community-driven: you are a participant, not a customer
noCompany.whatIs4: Permissionless: anyone can build on or use the protocol
noCompany.foundationIntro: A nonprofit that supports the ecosystem (reference apps, education, and coordination) but does not control the protocol.
noCompany.foundationTask1: Maintains the mobile app & explorer as reference implementations
noCompany.foundationTask2: Operates some public infrastructure (others can run theirs)
noCompany.foundationTask3: Coordinates community votes and discussions in the app (where applicable)
noCompany.foundationTask4: Publishes the Umotu Stable Accord and conservative default lists for the Umotu wallet (others can support other stables)
noCompany.foundationNote: The Foundation cannot access your wallet and cannot reverse transactions. Wallet defaults do not restrict what is possible on the protocol.

scamPrevention.title: Avoid Scams
scamPrevention.subtitle: Protect yourself
scamPrevention.intro: The Umotu Foundation will NEVER DM you. Anyone pretending to be 'support' is a scammer.
scamPrevention.redFlag1: Someone claiming to be customer support
scamPrevention.redFlag3: Requests for your 12-word phrase or private key
scamPrevention.redFlag4: Promises of bonus tokens if you send funds
scamPrevention.redFlag5: Apps or sites asking you to 'activate' rewards
scamPrevention.truth1: There is no customer support that can reset your wallet
scamPrevention.truth2: All official updates appear in this app
scamPrevention.truth3: No one from Umotu will ask for your recovery phrase or private key
scamPrevention.truth4: Download this app only from the official Apple App Store or Google Play

airdrop.title: Testnet Recognition
airdrop.subtitle: Early testnet participation may be recognized
airdrop.intro: Umotu is in testnet. Active testnet participants may receive a recognition allocation when mainnet launches. This is not guaranteed and may be zero.
airdrop.step1: Create a wallet in the app
airdrop.step3: Use the app regularly during testnet
airdrop.whyTestnet: Testnet is essential: users learn wallet security, and the system is tested for security, robustness, and efficiency before mainnet.
airdrop.timeline: Mainnet timing and any recognition details will be announced in the app.
airdrop.totalPool: Example only: if a 1,000,000,000 UMT recognition pool is created, your share depends on eligible participants and program rules. The earlier you participate, the higher your share.
airdrop.scenario2: Example only: 1M eligible users → ~1,000 UMT average each (before weighting)
airdrop.scenario3: Example only: 10M eligible users → ~100 UMT average each (before weighting)
airdrop.scenario4: Example only: 100M eligible users → ~10 UMT average each (before weighting)
airdrop.disclaimer: Examples only—not guarantees. Any recognition allocation depends on program rules and market conditions, and may be zero.
airdrop.reward: At mainnet, your testnet wallet may receive real tokens based on participation and program rules. Earlier and more active users are prioritized.
airdrop.note: Note: Testnet tokens have no value. Any mainnet allocation is determined by program policy at launch. All news about airdrop will be published only in this app.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.subtitle: Your first five minutes
gettingStarted.check3: 3) IMPORTANT: Write down your 12 words on paper
gettingStarted.check5: 5) Keep the app running to earn daily rewards
gettingStarted.tip1: Allow background access so checks can run
gettingStarted.tip2: Check back daily to see rewards
gettingStarted.tip3: Invite friends—more participants can make the network stronger

governance.title: Governance & Votes
governance.subtitle: How in-app voting works
governance.intro: Umotu supports in-app polls and votes so participants can help guide certain ecosystem decisions.
governance.whatVotesDo1: Recommend wallet defaults (for example, which stablecoins appear as preferred payout or stable-gas options)
governance.whatVotesDo2: Signal community preference on proposals and priorities
governance.whatVotesDo3: Approve certain grants or budgets where enabled
governance.whatVotesCannotDo1: Access your wallet, move your funds, or reset your recovery phrase
governance.whatVotesCannotDo2: Reverse transactions or freeze your self-custody wallet
governance.whatVotesCannotDo3: Change fixed monetary policy outside the protocol rules
governance.note: Some polls may be mock or advisory depending on the network stage. Always read the proposal details and activation conditions.

errors.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection.
errors.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

onboard_wallet_explain: Create or import a wallet to receive rewards.
wallet_backup_explain: Write down these 12 words in order. This is the ONLY way to recover your wallet if you lose your device.
wallet_backup_warning: Never share your recovery phrase. Anyone with these words can access your funds.
wallet_backup_confirm: I have written down my recovery phrase
wallet_save_first: Please save your recovery phrase first

seedReveal.title: Recovery Phrase
seedReveal.intro: Your recovery phrase is the only way to restore your wallet if you lose access to this device. Write it down and store it securely offline.
seedReveal.hiddenMsg: Your phrase is hidden for security. Tap below to reveal it.
seedReveal.noScreenshot: Never take a screenshot of your recovery phrase.
seedReveal.checkPrompt: Enter the words below to confirm you've written them down correctly:
seedReveal.checkIncorrect: One or more words are incorrect. Please check your backup.
seedReveal.ackWritten: I have written down my recovery phrase
seedReveal.ackCannotRecover: I understand Umotu cannot recover my wallet
